risks: If ignored, defoliation scales up leading to sunscald on fruit
Spreads rapidly in warm, rainy weather
Bacteria survive in seeds and crop debris
Bacterial_Spot.trigger_conditions: Temp approaches 30°C + Humidity nears 88% + Wet soil

risks: If ignored, causes significant fruit drop and yield loss
Affects lower leaves first and moves upward
Lesions on stems can girdle the plant
If ignored, absolute crop failure within a few days
Extremely infectious across entire farm
Makes all fruits rot with a foul odor
remedies: Apply Metalaxyl or systemic fungicides immediately
Destroy infected plants completely (do not compost)
Late_Blight.trigger_conditions: Cool temp < 20°C + Humidity > 94% + High soil moisture > 87%

risks: If ignored, older foliage dies prematurely
Stunts the fruit growth significantly
High transmission in greenhouse environments
remedies: Increase air flow through the greenhouse

risks: If ignored, rapid defoliation heavily reduces vigor
Sunscald damages exposed fruit
Survives winter on weeds and debris
remedies: Mulch base of plants to prevent soil splashing

risks: If ignored, fine webbing suffocates leaves and they collapse
Populations explode in hot, dry weather within days
Stunts overall growth completely
remedies: Apply insecticidal soap, neem oil, or an acaricide
Mist plants with water to increase humidity
Water heavily to reduce drought stress that attracts mites
Spider_Mites.trigger_conditions: Hot Temp > 33°C + DRY Humidity < 50% + DRY Soil < 50%

risks: If ignored, concentric dark lesions form on fruit making it unsellable
Leaves dry out and fall
Favors prolonged leaf wetness
remedies: Prune plants to ensure fast drying of foliage

risks: If ignored, plant ceases all standard growth
No marketable fruit will be produced
Whiteflies spread this constantly to nearby crops
remedies: No chemical cure exists for infected plants
You must uproot and destroy infected plants immediately
Control whiteflies using reflective mulches and insecticides

risks: If ignored, mottled leaves reduce yield entirely
Virus easily transferred via hands or tools during pruning
Can persist in dead soil debris for years
remedies: Wash hands and sterilize clippers with 10% bleach
Do NOT smoke near plants as tobacco carries identical viruses

risks: No risks present currently
Ensure conditions remain optimal
remedies: Perform routine checks for early signs
